mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly positive when UNRATE < 6.0 over a 3-year test period, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable industrial production indicates strong credit-fueled demand meeting inelastic supply, compressing input costs and boosting equity returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint momentum of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, consumer price inflation exceeding 235 accelerates Federal Reserve tightening expectations, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235 during tightening periods, or the signal yields positive returns in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust economic activity, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1000 units signals a cooling construction sector, reducing related corporate earnings and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST falling below 1000 does not lead to negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of cases over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion absorbing labor slack, boosting corporate profitability and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 for 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid real GDP growth exceeding 15700, rising consumer prices trigger stagflation fears, causing equity risk premiums to spike and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: The stagflation signal fails to predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 4-year test period.
